stimulus: One challenge when investigating whether attending a selective music conservatory shapes a musician’s later performance style is the problem of ensuring that the study includes an appropriate control group. To reveal the influence of conservatory training, researchers must compare musicians who graduated from a top conservatory with musicians who did not attend such a conservatory but who are otherwise comparable in talent, background, and early training. Because researchers cannot determine which applicants are admitted to elite conservatories in any given year, they therefore ______blank
stem: Which choice most logically completes the text?

answerOptions.content: struggle to obtain reliable performance data from musicians who never attended a selective conservatory.
can carry out informative studies only on musicians who have already retired from active performance.
will likely find it difficult to assemble a control group that meaningfully resembles conservatory graduates.
should deliberately select control-group musicians who differ in talent and background from the conservatory graduates.

rationale:
Choice C is the best answer. The text frames the central challenge as constructing a control group of non-attending musicians who match conservatory graduates in talent, background, and early training. Because researchers cannot decide who is admitted, they cannot directly engineer such a matched group, so identifying an appropriate control group will be hard.

Choice A is incorrect because the difficulty is in finding comparable non-attendees, not in obtaining their performance data. Choice B is incorrect because focusing on retired musicians would defeat the goal of studying training effects across active careers. Choice D is incorrect because deliberately picking dissimilar controls violates the very definition of an appropriate control group.
